questions.question: Who wrote the story 'The Lost Child'?
questions.answer: The story 'The Lost Child' was written by Mulk Raj Anand.

questions.question: Where was the child going with his parents?
questions.answer: The child was going to a fair with his parents.

questions.question: What festival was being celebrated in the story?
questions.answer: The festival of spring was being celebrated.

questions.question: Why did the child lag behind on the way to the fair?
questions.answer: The child lagged behind because he was fascinated by the toys and sights on the way.

questions.question: What field did the child see on the way to the fair?
questions.answer: The child saw a beautiful mustard field.

questions.question: Which insects did the child watch in the mustard field?
questions.answer: The child watched dragonflies and butterflies in the mustard field.

questions.question: Which sweet did the child want from the sweet shop?
questions.answer: The child wanted burfi from the sweet shop.

questions.question: What did the flower seller offer at the fair?
questions.answer: The flower seller offered a garland of gulmohur flowers.

questions.question: What colourful items attracted the child at the fair?
questions.answer: The colourful balloons attracted the child at the fair.

questions.question: What kind of entertainment was provided by the snake-charmer?
questions.answer: The snake-charmer played music on a flute for a snake.

questions.question: What ride did the child want to enjoy at the fair?
questions.answer: The child wanted to ride on the roundabout.

questions.question: When did the child realise he was lost?
questions.answer: The child realised he was lost when he could not find his parents near him.

questions.question: What did the child cry when he got lost?
questions.answer: The child cried loudly for his mother and father.

questions.question: How did the child feel after losing his parents?
questions.answer: The child felt frightened and helpless after losing his parents.

questions.question: Where did the child search for his parents?
questions.answer: The child searched for his parents in the crowded fair.

questions.question: Who helped the lost child in the crowd?
questions.answer: A kind man in the crowd helped the lost child.

questions.question: What did the man first offer the child?
questions.answer: The man first offered the child a ride on the roundabout.

questions.question: Why did the child refuse the roundabout ride?
questions.answer: The child refused because he wanted his parents.

questions.question: What other attractions did the man offer the child?
questions.answer: The man offered the child balloons, flowers, and sweets.

questions.question: Did the child accept any of the attractions offered by the man?
questions.answer: No, the child refused all the attractions.

questions.question: Why did the child refuse all the attractions?
questions.answer: The child refused them because he only wanted his parents.

questions.question: What does the child’s behaviour show about his parents?
questions.answer: It shows that he loved his parents deeply.

questions.question: What is the main theme of the story?
questions.answer: The main theme is the importance of parents and family love.

questions.question: What message does the story convey?
questions.answer: The story conveys that parental love and security are more important than material attractions.

questions.question: Why is the story called 'The Lost Child'?
questions.answer: It is called 'The Lost Child' because the child gets separated from his parents in the fair.
